concerns.analysis: Multiple meaningful charts and graphs from the source PDF failed to render as elements in the HTML output. Instead, they appear as literal text placeholders such as '[Image: ...]'. Because these images contain crucial data visualizations (e.g., maturity curves, discard ratios) that cannot be fully conveyed by the surrounding text alone, their absence constitutes a critical defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Figure 1.14 on page 134, a line graph showing a maturity curve.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Line graph showing a maturity curve (an S-shaped trend line). The x-axis represents Length (cm) from 22.5 to 72.5, and the y-axis represents Proportion Mature from 0.0 to 1.0. A legend indicates open circles are 'Observed' and the solid thick line is 'Predicted'.]

concerns.analysis: On page 30, the subheadings 'South Carolina', 'Georgia', and 'Florida' belong under the parent section '2.1.1.1 Survey Design and Methods' (which is level H4 on page 29). However, in the JSON output, these three subheadings are assigned level H2. This places them higher in the hierarchy than their parent section, reversing the containment relationship and breaking the heading structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
South Carolina
Commercial landings of southern flounder caught in South Carolina state waters must be sold...

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 11 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=11, examples=['http://www.st.nmfs.noaa.gov/recreational-fisheries/index', 'http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.201', 'https://www.st.nmfs.noaa.gov/Assets/Quality-Assurance/docume']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://github.com/cmlegault/ASAPplots
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 239 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (173 truncated mid-sentence, 66 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p21: "...uals 1 over the denominator 1 plus e raised to the"; p128: "...the relationship between "Age" on the x-axis (0 to"; p129: "...nship between Age on the x-axis, ranging from 0 to"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=239 (truncated=173, placeholder=66, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (422): ii; iii; iv
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=422. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 8 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=8. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
